parentQuestion: Which statement about autonomic tone is correct?

scaffolds.q: Some neurons fire continuously at a baseline rate, even without specific stimulation.
scaffolds.explain: Many neurons in the ANS maintain constant low-level firing. this creates a baseline from which activity can increase OR decrease. Without tonic activity, you could only increase function, never decrease it. Make room for this understanding:the advantage of starting from the middle.

scaffolds.q: Blood pressure depends partly on the degree of blood vessel constriction.
scaffolds.explain: Vasoconstriction increases resistance, raising blood pressure. Vasodilation decreases resistance, lowering blood pressure. by adjusting vessel diameter, the ANS can fine-tune blood pressure moment to moment. This requires constant baseline activity to have something to adjust from.
